data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Ground agent: the keeper of the project's human-readable mind. You own three things and must leave each true to the project's real state before you finish:
- the blueprints (LaTeX-subset declarations + `\uses` dependency edges),
- the roadmap (the planning index that drives scheduling) — edit it ONLY via the `"$HORIZON_BIN" roadmap` CLI (`set`/`add`/`remove`, `comment <id> --author ground` to log a key advance; pass long prose with `--summary-file` and `--author` on add/set), NEVER by hand-editing the YAML, which corrupts it; the CLI quotes safely,
- memory: durable facts/conventions/dead ends, kept as `memory` inbox items (`horizon inbox add --kind memory --author ground`); prune stale ones with `inbox complete`.
You do NOT do long Lean proof search yourself. You set strategy, keep the blueprints aligned with both the Lean code and the long-term plan, supervise the Horizon agent, and leave it a clear recommendation of what to attempt next (see the recommendation guidance).

# This is a RECONCILE Ground
A Horizon session just ran. Review its work via the project diff (the `project-git` skill) and its report appended below, then reconcile blueprints/roadmap/memory with what actually changed.

The Horizon agent runs free and may leave the workspace messy or its reasoning shaky — you are its supervisor and the workspace's janitor, and you make it correct and tidy again.

How to work:
- On a reconcile round, review the Horizon agent's latest work via its report and the project diff (see the `project-git` skill — projects have no root `.git`; the workspace keeps one out-of-tree ledger, so diff a project by pathspec / by the session's commit trailers). Check that the Lean definitions truly match the blueprint statements, that its reasoning is sound, and that it left no stray files or directories.
- Reconcile blueprints, roadmap status, and memory with what actually changed. Fix blueprint errors, retrieve and add missing references, and keep the DAG dependency-correct.
- When you find a flaw — a wrong proof, a Lean/blueprint mismatch, a dead end — raise an inbox item or comment rather than silently papering over it (see the horizon-inbox skill); use `--author ground` for inbox items/comments you create. ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, roadmap summaries, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. The inbox is durable coordination, not a progress log: prefer one concise issue/hint over many comments, close obsolete items, retag or merge duplicates, and add a concise closing comment when you `complete` an item.
- When you make a change the human should know about — you restructured projects, took an unexpected direction, or hit something worth flagging — tell them with an `info` inbox item (`horizon inbox add --kind info --author ground`). It is purely a notice and never affects what runs.
- You do not create tasks: tasks are the human's way to launch sessions (and are inferred on demand from a roadmap milestone via `horizon run <roadmap_id>`). To propose work, open an inbox item addressed to the human (`horizon inbox add --to human`); you may `horizon task comment` to suggest an edit, but never add/edit/remove tasks.
- Delegate to subagents to divide the work (blueprint review, diff analysis), preferring a smaller/cheaper model for mechanical checks.
- Keep the roadmap as the project's mathematical status: the main theorems and infrastructure already formalized and still to build, each a milestone that guides the work — it is a map, not a task queue. Update item statuses to match the real Lean/blueprint state, and add milestones for major results that emerge. Before closing/blocking/rejecting a roadmap item, add a concise roadmap comment explaining the evidence and checks. If you comment on a task whose status should be changed by the human, explicitly name the linked `roadmap_refs` that should move with it and the matching status/comment to apply. Marking an item active is guidance, not a command, and does not by itself launch work: a human runs a milestone with `horizon run <roadmap_id>`. If you think work should start, say so in an inbox item to the human.
- Treat the opened inbox below as input for this round only. Preserve frozen/protected state; if an edit would violate it, leave it and flag it.

Blueprint constraints (legible and pure, but mathematically complete — see the blueprint-conventions skill):
- One declaration's worth per node: a short statement + a COMPLETE proof (not a sketch). Keep nodes small by splitting a hard step into its own `\uses`-linked lemma, never by abbreviating the proof to a hand-wave.
- Pure mathematics only: no Lean tactics, no semi-Lean pseudocode, no project history. Do not restate Lean source as prose. Add a `\uses` edge rather than re-explaining a dependency.
- The roadmap is an index, not a journal: one line of summary per item; link, don't recount.
- Memory holds only what is non-obvious and durable: conventions, invariants, dead ends.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Run: 0006; focus: tasks=T6

- SubProjects/Albanese/**
- .archon-horizon/roadmap/**
- .archon-horizon/blueprints/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log directory for this session: .archon-horizon/runs/0006/sessions/0017-ground

# Roadmap
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends over codimension-$1$ points:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
`CodimOneExtension` (×3), `Thm32RationalMapExtension` (×1).

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III §6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (×7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.

- CECH.acyclic [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing) - **Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing).**
$$\check{H}^{i}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})=0\ (i>0)\quad\text{on affine opens},$$
via tilde-vanishing $\Rightarrow$ affine Čech vanishing; localization comparison `AwayComparison`, $\varphi/\varphi_L$ naturality.

- CECH.engine [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Combinatorial / free Čech engine - **Combinatorial / free Čech engine (sorry-free).** Alternating coface complex, homotopy contraction, and exactness of the augmented Čech complex on a cover.

- CECH.main [done/proof/high] (Cech-Cohomology) cech_computes_higherDirectImage (unconditional) - **Comparison theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`.**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}$$
for separated quasi-compact $f$ and a finite affine cover — unconditional (no enough-injectives appeal). Deliverable merged into AJC.

- CECH.pushpull [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) PushPull functoriality - **PushPull functoriality.** `pushPullMap` composition, leg coherence, and the pentagon for the sigma iso over the Čech nerve / wide pullbacks (finitary-extensive distributivity).

- GRQ.cells [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent - **Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent (sorry-free).** Rank-quotient setoid, affine charts, the $\mathrm{GL}_{d}$ transition cocycle, and effective descent gluing the charts into $\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)$.

- GRQ.graded [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Section graded ring (SNAP) - **Section graded ring (SNAP, sorry-free).** The graded ring/module lanes (Stacks 01CV) and graded Hilbert–Serre rationality:
$$\sum_{n}\dim_{k}M_{n}\,t^{n}\in\mathbb{Z}(t)\ \text{(rational)}.$$

- GRQ.quot [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent) - **Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent, sorry-free).** The quasi-coherent-descent machinery of `QuotScheme` retained after carving the χ-blocked Hilbert-polynomial endgame (which stays open in the AJC copy).

- GRQ.represents [done/proof/high] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian.represents (H^0 leg) - **Representability `Grassmannian.represents` (H^0 leg).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\ \text{represents}\ T\mapsto\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{rank }d\,\},\qquad \texttt{tautologicalQuotient\_epi}.$$

- LBC.compinv [done/proof/high] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Terminal comparison inverse (Pic^sharp group law) - **Terminal comparison inverse (deliverable — finished).** The keystone `trivialisation_restrict_compat` and the chart-chase inverse, giving the comparison isomorphism that endows $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ with its group structure. Merges back into AJC (**T1**).

- LBC.dual [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route) - **Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route, sorry-free).**
$$\mathcal{L}^{\vee}|_{U}\;\cong\;(\mathcal{L}|_{U})^{\vee}$$
compatibly across the trivializing slices — `DualInverse`, `DualInverse/SliceTransport`.

- LBC.pullback [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor - **Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor (sorry-free).** `LineBundlePullback`, `RelPicFunctor`; the seed compatibility $f^{*}(\mathcal L\otimes\mathcal M)\cong f^{*}\mathcal L\otimes f^{*}\mathcal M$ on locally trivial bundles (`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv`).

- LBC.stalktensor [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery - **Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery (sorry-free).** `TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor`, `PresheafInternalHom` — the local model for $\otimes$ and $\mathcal{H}om$ on quasi-coherent sheaves used by the comparison.

- MR.pic [pending/blueprint/normal] (MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard) Compactifying the Picard scheme (D'Souza) - **Compactifying the Picard scheme** (D'Souza, *MR0555258*). Blueprint-stage related-paper formalization: the compactified Jacobian / degenerate Picard variety of a singular curve. Depends on the core AG engine (schemes, cohomology, Picard). Lean target is currently a stub aggregator.

- PIC.idcomp [blocked/proof/normal] (Picard-IdentityComponent) Identity-component group-scheme substrate (A.3 leg) - **Identity-component group-scheme substrate (A.3 leg).** Extraction subproject carrying the `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent`-based construction of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}$ and its abelian-variety structure:
$$e\in\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=\big(\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\big)^{\!\circ},\qquad \dim_k\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g .$$
Builds green (byte-identical to the AJC copies) as an FBC-free, isolated iteration baseline. All 9 residual `sorry` are **substrate-blocked**, not proof-search: the §1 group-scheme leaves (identity-component clopen / subgroup / base change) need Stacks **037Q/04KU** (connected $k$-scheme + $k$-rational section $\Rightarrow$ geometrically connected) and **02LB/04KS** clopen-descent, both absent from v4.31 Mathlib; the $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$-specific leaves (`Pic0Scheme`, `degree`, `isAbelianVariety`, `finrank_eq_genus`) inherit the FGA sorry-taint (`AJC.picrep`) and the tangent-space comparison (`AJC.pic0av`).

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- **Proj / non-affine case** (ℙ¹): cover the base change by pulled-back chart opens. Per chart: `Proj.awayι_toSpecZero_assoc` + `← Spec.map_comp` + `change` collapses `awayι ≫ hom` to `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`, then the affine recipe. Covering: `Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top` with the (now named) `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span`. Intersection: any preimage of the generic point `⟨⊥, Ideal.isPrime_bot, …⟩` under `pullback.fst` (surjective — `Surjective` is `IsStableUnderBaseChange`, `MorphismProperty.pullback_fst`). **Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker`; quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
- k̄-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ⧸ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) — installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ⧸ m)` synthesis (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does).

**SUPERSEDED for the all-points case (run 0006 T6): Stacks 00OF is NO LONGER needed.** The old claim here — "remaining genuine gap for all-points `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` is Stacks 00OF, do not attempt" — is now false. See [[I-0047]]: `Albanese/SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean` proves regularity at EVERY prime of a standard-smooth algebra over a perfect field, Serre-free (trdeg–height induction via Stacks 00ON + conormal split-exactness from `FormallySmooth.of_perfectField`), so `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` / `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` are now axiom-clean. - **Pipeline**: `f.fromFunctionField : Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` + Mathlib `IsFractionRing (stalk z) K(X)` (integral schemes) + DVR stalk ⇒ `ValuativeCommSq Y.hom`; extract the lift from `IsProper.eq_valuativeCriterion` (`rw at hP; exact hP.1.1.1`); spread out with `PartialMap.ofFromSpecStalk X.hom Y.hom L hfacr` (`[X.IsGermInjectiveAt z]` automatic for integral) + `mem_domain_ofFromSpecStalk`; identify with `f` via `RationalMap.eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq` + a small glue lemma `fromFunctionField = Spec.map (stalkSpecializes gen⤳z) ≫ fromSpecStalkOfMem` (prove by `cancel_mono g.domain.ι` + `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` + `SpecMap_stalkSpecializes_fromSpecStalk`).

Materialise them OUTSIDE the literal and pass as explicit fields (`domain := hdom` etc.).

Push it to the function field via `exists_rep` + `compHom_toRationalMap` + simp with `fromSpecStalkOfMem_compHom/toPartialMap`.

Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.

Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.

# Recommendation for the next agent (write `recommendation.md`)
Leave ONE short, concrete handoff aimed ONLY at the next Ground/Horizon session — not the human, not a status recap. Say what to attempt next and where: the specific blueprint node / lemma / file to work on, the tactic or approach to try, and the pitfalls or dead ends to avoid. Keep it actionable (a few bullets); anything the human should know goes to an `info` inbox item instead, and durable dead ends go to memory.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Ground session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

Everything is clean. The working-tree state matches session start (untracked-at-root as before), scratch removed, and the scope files are at their target state.

- Deleted the three dead `CodimOneFree` helpers in Thm32; added `av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` (3.3 + codim-≥2 ⇒ `Z(f) = ∅`).
- Blueprint reconciled: `thm:codim_one_extension` restated/repinned (fully `\leanok`), new `cor:codim_one_free` and counterexample remark, Thm32 nodes/`\uses` rewired, phantom 0AVF/Auslander–Buchsbaum "Step 2" dependency purged, `lean_decls` updated.

name: project-git
description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
---

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** — its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.

data.content:
======================================================================
I-0065 memory to=None
TITLE: None
Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
======================================================================
I-0064 info to=None
TITLE: None
T6 session 0015: false Milne-3.1 "extension" statement removed; Thm 3.2 chain refactored, now gated ONLY on Milne 3.3. Albanese green.

**Soundness finding.** `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (`CodimOneExtension.lean`, sorry) claimed: a codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map from a nonsingular variety to a **complete** variety extends to a regular morphism. This is **false**: the projection $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$, $[x{:}y{:}z]\mapsto[x{:}y]$, has indeterminacy exactly one codim-2 point (so is CodimOneFree) but admits no regular extension — any extension $g$ would force $\mathrm{Dom}(f)=\mathbb P^2$ via `PartialMap.le_domain_toRationalMap`. The `sorry` was unclosable; Milne 3.1 asserts only codim-≥2 indeterminacy, and the everywhere-extension needs the *group-variety* input (Lemma 3.3) to first empty the locus.

**Refactor (all built + axiom-checked, `lake build` green, checkdecls unchanged):**

- NEW proved `existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`: $Z(f)=\emptyset$ ⇒ unique regular representative (any reduced source / separated target; via Mathlib's maximal representative `RationalMap.toPartialMap`), + helper `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (morphisms with equal rational class are equal) and simp lemma `toPartialMap_domain`. All axiom-clean.
- NEW proved `codimOneFree_of_smooth_of_complete` (Milne 3.1's CodimOneFree phrasing, no 3.3 dependency) — also closes hint `I-0059`.
- Milne 3.3 statement **strengthened**: the codim-1 witness must lie in $Z(f)$ (old form was nearly vacuous and could not force emptiness). Still the chapter's single `sorry`.
- `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`: three `CodimOneFree` helpers replaced by `av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` (3.3 + codim-≥2 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$); `extend_to_av` = emptiness + extension theorem, sorry-free body, `sorryAx` only via 3.3.
- Blueprint: `thm:codim_one_extension` restated + repinned (now fully `\leanok`), new `cor:codim_one_free`, `rmk:codim1_no_extension_from_codim1free` (the counterexample), Thm32 chapter nodes/`\uses` rewired, stale 0AVF/Auslander–Buchsbaum "Step 2" prose removed (AB is no longer consumed by this chain at all); `lean_decls` updated. `horizon blueprint` refreshed: 299 nodes, 289 proved, 0 dangling.

**Ledger:** `CodimOneExtension.lean` sorries 2 → 1 (only Milne 3.3, `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`). The 0AVF / Stacks depth-≥2 gap is **gone from the roadmap** for ALB.codim1 — it was only ever needed for the false statement.
======================================================================
I-0049 issue to=None
TITLE: None
[bp-review T6] Blueprint changes for SmoothPrimeRegularity + indeterminacy_codimGe2 are mathematically sound; two minor findings to tidy.

CORE VERDICT: PASS. The 7 new nodes in subsec:smooth_prime_regularity all have correct 1-to-1 Lean pins (Polynomial.step_height_trdeg_of_isPrime, MvPolynomial.exists_le_trdeg_and_natCard_le_height_add, Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_le_trdeg_and_natCast_le_height_add, Algebra.rank_kaehlerDifferential_eq_trdeg, finrank_cotangentSpace_add_finrank_kaehler_residueField, isRegularLocalRing_of_isLocalization_atPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_perfectField). SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean and StandardSmoothDimension.lean are both sorry-free and compiled (oleans present); signatures match the LaTeX (hypotheses, trdeg/height directions, conormal identity, perfect-field/EssFiniteType assumptions all correct). All \uses targets on changed nodes resolve; no dangling refs to the ~9 removed MvPolynomial/submersive substrate labels. The rewritten smooth_to_regular_local_ring proof is now pure and routes correctly through thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime.

LEANOK HONESTY: correct. thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2 pins indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete (CodimOneExtension.lean:1374, body = sorry); node marks \leanok on the STATEMENT line only, proof block has NO \leanok. Same for thm:codim_one_extension (Lean sorry, not \leanok). Honest per statement-level convention.

FINDING 1 (math-purity, minor): The rewritten narrative subsections in Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex (subsec:codim1_substrate_lemmas and the 'superseded decomposition' paragraph) still leak implementation/project history in ADDED lines: commit hash 'b80f227', 'proved axiom-clean', 'removed as dead code', Lean filename 'StandardSmoothDimension.lean'. The run greatly reduced this (-894 lines) but did not fully purge it. Non-node prose; pre-existing pattern.

FINDING 2 (dead placeholder pins, minor): Now off the critical path (dropped from smooth_to_regular_local_ring \uses), the two nodes lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly and lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula have \lean{} pins (AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.stage6_regular_stalk_assembly and Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension) that do NOT resolve to any Lean declaration (absent from source at HEAD and now; only listed in blueprint/lean_decls). They do not create dangling DAG edges and are correctly NOT \leanok, but they break 1-to-1 correspondence (pin -> nonexistent decl) and are superseded by the arbitrary-prime route. Candidates for removal.

NOTE (not a finding): thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime LaTeX says 'standard-smooth of relative dimension n' while the Lean uses [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S] with n derived internally; conclusion unaffected, acceptable.
======================================================================
I-0063 info to=None
TITLE: None
**AJC working tree is shared by ≥3 concurrent runs; no full green build was confirmed this session.**

At reconcile time the AJC project dir had live builds from OTHER runs racing in it (olean-corruption risk, per the standing warning):

- Run 0007 session `0007-horizon-T8`: a full `lake build` (~40+ min in).
- A T2 build of `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1` (the ~70-min leg).

The uncommitted working tree therefore mixes several runs work, not just T5 s:

- **T5 (this reconcile):** `RelPicFunctor.lean` + `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex` — reviewed, sound, axiom-clean per the session report.
- **Not T5 (in-flight, untouched by me):** `SectionGradedRing.lean` (+717, written ~2 min before my session — active T8 edit), `GrassmannianQuot.lean`, `QuotScheme.lean`, `GlueDescent.lean`, `Cech*.lean`, and untracked `Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean` (a real T2 FBC deliverable, never checkpointed).

**Consequence:** I did NOT run a confirming `lake build` (would race the live builds and risk corrupting shared oleans). The RelPicFunctor advance is verified only by diff review + the session s own axiom check, not by an independent kernel build. `SectionGradedRing` (the I-0001 v4.31 errors) is mid-repair by T8 and its state is unknown.

This escalates the standing `I-0016` concurrency concern. Suggest serializing AJC-mutating runs, or letting the T8/T2 builds finish and running one clean full `lake build` before the next integration commit.
======================================================================
I-0062 issue to=None
TITLE: None
**Blueprint pin drift: headline relative-Pic nodes carry `\leanok` but their pins compute the ABSOLUTE Picard group.**

In `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex`, three `\leanok` nodes describe the *relative* functor `T ↦ Pic(C×_kT)/π_T^*Pic(T)` but pin Lean decls that compute the *absolute* `Pic(C×_kT)` (iso-class carrier):

- `def:rel_pic_sharp` → `PicSharp`
- `lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial` → `PicSharp.functorial`
- `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` → `PicSharp.presheaf`
- (and `def:etale_sheaf` / `etSheaf_group_structure` sheafify the absolute presheaf)

Each node carries a transparent `%`-NOTE flagging the drift, and the honest relative counterparts now exist (`subsec:relpic_relative_functor`: `relPresheaf`, `relFunctorial`, `toRelPresheaf`, all axiom-clean). So this is a **`\leanok` overclaim**, not a math gap.

**Clean fix is coordinated Lean+blueprint work, not a blueprint-only edit:**
- Repinning the headline nodes to the relative family needs a `relEtSheaf` built on `relPresheaf` (only `relPresheaf`+`toRelPresheaf` exist; the étale-sheaf layer is still on the absolute `presheaf`).
- Blocked on the étale-topology decision (parametrize `J` vs Kleiman §2 `k`-rational-section route) — same blocker as the FGA `picSharp` rewire.

Do NOT repin piecemeal (def→relative while functorial/presheaf stay absolute) — that trades one inconsistency for another. See `AJC.picrep` roadmap comment and `I-0060`.

42	\section{Setup and motivation}
43	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
44	
45	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
46	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
47	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
48	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
49	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
50	
51	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
52	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
53	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
54	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
55	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
56	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
57	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
58	
59	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
60	\begin{quote}
61	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
62	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
63	\end{quote}
64	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
65	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
66	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
67	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
68	in our setting are:
69	\begin{itemize}
70	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
71	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
72	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
73	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
74	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
75	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
76	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
77	\end{itemize}
78	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
79	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
80	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
81	\cref{thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2} is Milne~3.1 (with corollary
82	\cref{cor:codim_one_free} and the formal extension theorem
83	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}), and
84	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
85	\texttt{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
86	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
87	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
88	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
89	
90	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
91	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
92	
93	\begin{definition}
94	
95	
96	\leanok
97	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
98	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
99	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
100	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let \(V\) and \(W\)
101	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
102	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
103	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi\) is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
104	  % \(v\) of \(V\) if \(v \in U\) for some \((U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi\). The set \(U_1\) of
105	  % \(v\) at which \(\varphi\) is defined is open, and there is a regular map
106	  % \(\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W\) such that \((U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi\) --- clearly,
107	  % \(U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U\) and we can define \(\varphi_1\) to be
108	  % the regular map such that \(\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U\) for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
109	  % \varphi$."
110	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
111	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
112	  The \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\) is the open set
113	  \[
114	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
115	  \]
116	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The
117	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of \(f\) is the closed complement
118	  \[
119	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
120	  \]
121	  The map \(f\) is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset\).
122	
123	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
124	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} is a method on the
125	  Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure pinned at
126	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; it returns a
127	  \texttt{Set X} (the underlying carrier of the closed complement of
128	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain}, which is already exposed by Mathlib). The
129	  closedness of \(Z(f)\) is a separate \texttt{IsClosed} witness, packaged either as a
130	  field of \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} or as a standalone lemma
131	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus}.
132	\end{definition}
133	
134	
135	\begin{definition}
136	
137	
138	\leanok
139	  [Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps]
140	  \label{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}
141	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree}
142	  \uses{def:indeterminacy_locus, def:prime_divisor}
143	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 --- the conclusion
144	  % "an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)"
145	  % is the standing predicate we abstract (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf,
146	  % PDF page 22).
147	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow W\) from a normal
148	  % variety \(V\) to a complete variety \(W\) is defined on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose
149	  % complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)."
150	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 (the
151	  conclusion).}
152	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
153	  We say \(f\) is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-\(\geq 2\)
154	  indeterminacy}) if every irreducible component of the closed subset \(Z(f) \subseteq X\)
155	  has codimension \(\geq 2\) in \(X\). Equivalently, no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in
156	  \(Z(f)\); equivalently, every point \(\eta \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\)
157	  lies in \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).
158	
159	  \paragraph{Why the abstraction.} Milne's Theorem~3.1 (\cref{thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2}
160	  below) and Lemma~3.3 (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} below) both produce
161	  conclusions about the codimension of \(Z(f)\). Phrasing the codim-\(\geq 2\) condition
162	  as a named predicate keeps the cited statements concise and lets the consumer
163	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} read off the combination
164	  (\(Z(f)\) has codim \(\geq 2\) from \cref{thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2} \emph{and} \(Z(f)\) is
165	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} \(\Rightarrow\) \(Z(f)\)
166	  is empty) as a single line.
167	
168	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
169	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} is a \texttt{Prop}-valued
170	  predicate, encoded via Mathlib's \texttt{Order.coheight} on the specialisation
171	  preorder of \texttt{X.carrier} as in
172	  \cref{def:prime_divisor}: \(f\) is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if and only if for every
173	  \(\eta \in X\) with \(\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1\), \(\eta \in
174	  \texttt{f.domain}\). The encoding matches the standing \(\mathrm{Order.coheight}\)
175	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of \(X\) and the
176	  hypothesis ``no codim-\(1\) indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.
177	\end{definition}
178	
179	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
180	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
181	
182	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
183	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
184	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
185	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
186	
187	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
188	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
189	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
190	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
191	
192	\subsection{K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate}
193	\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}
194	
195	The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
196	(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) is assembled from several
197	substrate results: smoothness gives flatness at a stalk and, locally, a
198	standard-smooth presentation; the scheme-to-algebra bridge and the freeness of
199	\(\Omega_{S/R}\) for a standard-smooth algebra \(R \to S\) then reduce the
200	regularity claim to a freeness-plus-rank computation on K\"ahler differentials,
201	transported through localisation at the prime defining the stalk. The two
202	localisation helpers below --- transport of freeness, and matching of rank under
203	localisation --- supply this reduction. The Krull-dimension and cotangent inputs
204	are collected in \cref{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}, and the regularity
205	conclusion at an arbitrary prime is \cref{thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime}.
206	
207	The substrate lemmas are recorded below, feeding
208	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}.
209

216	  stalks is flat.

280	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}

282	  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth}

284	  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
285	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
286	  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that

318	  the algebra-side packaging of Stacks tag \texttt{00T7}(2)) and that
319	  the localisation \(S_M\) is non-trivial as a ring. Then the rank of
320	  \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) over \(S_M\) equals \(n\).

324	  Mathlib's
325	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak rank\_kaehlerDifferential}
326	  asserts \(\mathrm{rank}_S(\Omega_{S/R}) = n\) under the
327	  standard-smooth hypothesis. The localisation map
328	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) is an

339	\subsection{Stage 6 sub-decomposition: 00OE + 02JK chain}
340	\label{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}
341	
342	Given a standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart \(R \to S\) at a
343	point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\) and
344	\(\Omega_{S_\mathfrak q / R}\) free of rank \(n\) on the stalk
345	\(S_\mathfrak q = \mathcal O_{X, z}\), one concludes that \(S_\mathfrak q\)
346	is a regular local ring from two ingredients: (6.A) the smooth-algebra
347	Krull-dimension formula (Stacks tag \texttt{00OE}), and (6.B) the cotangent /
348	K\"ahler-differential bridge over a field base (Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}). The
349	two ingredients combine (6.C) into the regularity conclusion via
350	\(\dim S_\mathfrak q = \mathrm{rank}_{S_\mathfrak q}\Omega_{S_\mathfrak
351	q / R} = \dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\),
352	the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity. The next three lemmas
353	record these three ingredients.
354	
355	\begin{lemma}
356	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
357	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
358	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
359	        lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal,
360	        lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal,
361	        lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization,
362	        lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent,
363	        lem:exists_submersivePresentation_of_standardSmoothReldim}
364	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
365	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
366	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
367	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
368	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
369	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
370	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
371	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
372	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
373	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
374	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
375	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
376	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
377	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
378	  \[
379	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
380	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
381	  \]
382	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
383	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
384	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
385	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
386	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
387	
388	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the standard-smooth
389	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
390	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\),
391	  and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
392	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
393	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
394	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
395	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
396	\end{lemma}
397	
398	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \texttt{lemma-dimension-prime-polynomial-ring}
399	% we know that $r = \text{trdeg}_k\ \kappa(\mathfrak p)$ is equal to the
400	% dimension of $V(\mathfrak p)$. Pick any maximal chain of primes
401	% $\mathfrak p \subset \mathfrak p_1 \subset \ldots \subset \mathfrak p_r$
402	% starting with $\mathfrak p$ in $S$. This has length $r$ by Lemma
403	% \texttt{lemma-dimension-spell-it-out}. Let $\mathfrak q_j$, $j \in J$ be
404	% the minimal primes of $S$ which are contained in $\mathfrak p$. These
405	% correspond $1-1$ to minimal primes in $S_{\mathfrak p}$ via the rule
406	% $\mathfrak q_j \mapsto \mathfrak q_jS_{\mathfrak p}$. By Lemma
407	% \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-over-field} we know that
408	% $\dim_x(X)$ is equal to the maximum of the dimensions of the rings
409	% $S/\mathfrak q_j$. For each $j$ pick a maximal chain of primes
410	% $\mathfrak q_j \subset \mathfrak p'_1 \subset \ldots \subset
411	% \mathfrak p'_{s(j)} = \mathfrak p$. Then $\dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) =
412	% \max_{j \in J} s(j)$. Now, each chain
413	% $\mathfrak q_j \subset \mathfrak p'_1 \subset \ldots \subset
414	% \mathfrak p'_{s(j)} = \mathfrak p \subset \mathfrak p_1 \subset \ldots
415	% \subset \mathfrak p_r$ is a maximal chain in $S/\mathfrak q_j$, and by
416	% what was said before we have $\dim_x(X) = \max_{j \in J} r + s(j)$.
417	% The lemma follows."
418	\begin{proof}
419	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
420	        lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal,
421	        lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal,
422	        lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization,
423	        lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent,
424	        lem:exists_submersivePresentation_of_standardSmoothReldim}
425	  This is exactly Stacks tag~\texttt{00OE}
426	  (\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}); the proof is a
427	  citation of the dimension theory of finitely generated algebras over a
428	  field. By Noether normalisation the finite-type \(k\)-algebra \(S\) is
429	  finite over a polynomial subalgebra, so the going-up / catenary
430	  dimension theory of such algebras applies. Write
431	  \(r = \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); this transcendence
432	  degree is exactly the dimension of the closed subvariety
433	  \(\overline{\{x\}} = V(\mathfrak q)\). A maximal chain of primes
434	  through \(\mathfrak q\) therefore splits into a
435	  length-\(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) part below \(\mathfrak q\) (recording
436	  the local codimension, i.e.\ the height of \(\mathfrak q\) in the
437	  relevant irreducible component) and a length-\(r\) part above it
438	  (recording the dimension of \(\overline{\{x\}}\)). Taking the maximum
439	  over the minimal primes contained in \(\mathfrak q\) yields
440	  \[
441	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + r
442	    = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q),
443	  \]
444	  which is the asserted formula.
445	
446	  For the geometric specialisation matching the Lean target, take
447	  \(k = \bar k\) algebraically closed and \(S\) standard smooth of
448	  relative dimension \(n\), so that \(\dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = n\) via
449	  \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (the free
450	  K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\) has rank \(n\), which
451	  pins the relative dimension). The formula then gives
452	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak
453	  q)\). At a closed point \(\mathfrak q\) is maximal, so by the
454	  Nullstellensatz \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\) and
455	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\); hence
456	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = n\).
457	\end{proof}

459	\begin{lemma}
460	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
461	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
462	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
463	  \uses{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
464	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-differential-seq (canonically
465	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
466	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
467	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
468	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
469	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\texttt{equation-functorial-omega}),
470	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
471	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
472	  % $S'$-modules
473	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
474	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
475	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
476	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
477	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
478	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
479	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
480	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
481	  % (see Lemma \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
482	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
483	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
484	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
485	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
486	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
487	  \[
488	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
489	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
490	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
491	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
492	  \]
493	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
494	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
495	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
496	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
497	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
498	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
499	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
500	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
501	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
502	  \[
503	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
504	  \]
505	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
506	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
507	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
508	\end{lemma}

589	\begin{lemma}
590	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
591	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
592	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
593	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
594	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
595	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
596	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
597	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
598	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
599	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
600	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
601	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
602	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
603	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
604	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
605	  dimension equals \(1\).
606	\end{lemma}
607	
608	\begin{proof}
609	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
610	  \begin{enumerate}
611	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
612	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
613	      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
614	    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
615	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
616	      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
617	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
618	      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
619	      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
620	      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
621	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
622	      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
623	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
624	      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
625	      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
626	      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
627	      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
628	      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
629	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
630	      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
631	    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
632	      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
633	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
634	      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
635	      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
636	      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
637	      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
638	      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
639	      regular. The scheme-level transfer
640	      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3
641	      \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} on the affine chart) completes
642	      the conclusion.
643	  \end{enumerate}
644	\end{proof}

646	\subsection{Stacks 00OE closed-point substrate (superseded decomposition)}
647	\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}
648	
649	An earlier decomposition of Stage 6.A
650	(\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}, Stacks 00OE) reduced the
651	localised Krull-dimension formula at a closed point to a
652	Jacobian-regular-sequence witness for the relations of a submersive
653	presentation (Stacks 00SW / 00OW together with Matsumura Thm.~14.2). That
654	reduction is not needed: the closed-point case is proved unconditionally by the
655	Krull-height-theorem route ---
656	full height of maximal ideals of polynomial rings
657	(\cref{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}), the height
658	lower bound at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras
659	(\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}), the localised
660	dimension lower bound
661	(\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}), and the
662	cotangent upper bound
663	(\cref{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}) --- which needs no
664	regular-sequence witness. The Matsumura regular-sequence lemma itself
665	survives as \cref{lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent}.
666	The general (non-closed) point case does not require the
667	localisation theorem for regular local rings (Stacks \texttt{00OF}) at all:
668	regularity at an arbitrary prime is obtained directly, Serre-free, by the
669	trdeg--height route of \cref{subsec:smooth_prime_regularity}
670	(\cref{thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime}).
