=== main theorem statement in SmoothPrimeRegularity ===
theorem isRegularLocalRing_of_isLocalization_atPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_perfectField
    {k : Type u} [Field k] [PerfectField k]
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra k S]
    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S]
    (q : Ideal S) (hq : q.IsPrime)
    (Sq : Type u) [CommRing Sq] [IsLocalRing Sq] [Algebra k Sq] [Algebra S Sq]
    [IsScalarTower k S Sq] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sq q] :
    IsRegularLocalRing Sq := by
  haveI := hq
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing S := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing k S
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Sq := IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing q.primeCompl Sq ‹_›
  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ : ∃ n, Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S := by
    obtain ⟨ι, σ, _, _, ⟨P⟩⟩ := (inferInstance : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S).out
    exact ⟨P.dimension, P.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension rfl⟩
  haveI := hn
  -- Kähler package at the localisation (free of rank `n`).
  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄k]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule q.primeCompl (KaehlerDifferential.map k k S Sq) :=
    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map k S Sq q.primeCompl
  haveI : Module.Free Sq (Ω[Sq⁄k]) :=
    Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sq) (S := q.primeCompl)
      (M := Ω[S⁄k]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sq⁄k]) (KaehlerDifferential.map k k S Sq)
  have hrank : Module.rank Sq (Ω[Sq⁄k]) = n := by
    have h := Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sq q.primeCompl
      (KaehlerDifferential.map k k S Sq)
    rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
      (S := S) n] at h
    simpa using h
  -- Formal smoothness of `Sq` and of its residue field over `k`.
  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth S Sq := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization q.primeCompl
  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth k Sq := Algebra.FormallySmooth.comp k S Sq
  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType k S := Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_finiteType k S
  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType S Sq := Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_isLocalization Sq q.primeCompl
  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType k Sq := Algebra.EssFiniteType.comp k S Sq
  have hResSurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap Sq (ResidueField Sq)) := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType Sq (ResidueField Sq) :=
    Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective (Algebra.ofId Sq (ResidueField Sq)) hResSurj

text:
Filed as `I-0066`. Review complete.

## Verdict: CONVERGING — genuine progress, no defect found

Independent review of session `0015-horizon-T6`'s rewire of the Milne §I.3 chain. The work is real, not faked; the core claim holds up.

### Findings against the four focus points

1. **Removal justified.** `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` ("CodimOneFree ⇒ extension for any complete target") was genuinely false. The `ℙ² ⇢ ℙ¹` projection-from-a-point counterexample is correct — indeterminacy is a single codim-2 point (so `CodimOneFree` holds), yet no regular extension exists. This is a legitimate removal of an unclosable `sorry`, not abandonment of a hard-but-true lemma. Milne 3.1's honest content (codim-≥2 only) is now stated.

2. **New lemmas are real and non-vacuous.** `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` is a substantial (~90 LOC) valuative-criterion proof; `existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq`, `codimOneFree_of_smooth_of_complete`, `toPartialMap_domain` are all correctly typed real proofs. No circular use of Milne 3.3 (3.3 sits downstream).

3. **Chain is logically valid.** Milne 3.3's strengthening (`z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f`) is present and load-bearing — `av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` applies `codimGe2` to that `z` to derive `2 ≤ 1`, forcing `Z(f)=∅`, and `extend_to_av` then extends. Once 3.3 is proved, `extend_to_av` truly follows.

4. **No Lean/report mismatch or hidden sorry.** The sole `sorry` is Milne 3.3 (`CodimOneExtension.lean:1709`). No project-local `axiom`, no `admit`/`native_decide`/`decide`/`proof_wanted`/cheat tokens in any changed file.

### Notable under-reporting
The session also closed a **second, harder** `sorry` (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` — smooth ⇒ regular at every prime, previously the Stacks-00OF gap) via a new untracked 768-line file `SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean` (trdeg/height + Kähler-rank chain, imports only Mathlib + StandardSmoothDimension, no cycle, no sorry/axiom). This is disclosed in the report but under-emphasized relative to the false-statement headline; it is a large load-bearing claim worth an explicit `#print axioms` confirmation once the build settles.

### Heads-up (not a defect)
`extend_to_av` and the three Milne-§I.3 theorems gained the over-k̄ hypothesis `hf/hover : f.compHom Y.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap` — mathematically required and faithful to Milne, but a public-API change downstream `AlbaneseUP.lean` callers must thread (the report's Next already flags this).

I did not re-run `lake build`/`lean_verify`: a build for `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` (which shares the `AlgebraicJacobian` library) is running, so I avoided an olean race. The verdict rests on source review + token sweep; the report claims a full green build (8582 jobs) and clean `#print axioms`.

589	\begin{lemma}
590	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
591	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
592	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
593	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
594	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
595	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
596	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
597	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
598	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
599	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
600	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
601	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
602	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
603	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
604	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
605	  dimension equals \(1\).
606	\end{lemma}
607	
608	\begin{proof}

input.old_string:
\subsection{Stacks 00OE closed-point substrate (superseded decomposition)}
\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}

An earlier decomposition of Stage 6.A
(\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}, Stacks 00OE) reduced the
localised Krull-dimension formula at a closed point to a
Jacobian-regular-sequence witness for the relations of a submersive
presentation (Stacks 00SW / 00OW together with Matsumura Thm.~14.2). That
reduction is not needed: the closed-point case is proved unconditionally by the
Krull-height-theorem route ---
full height of maximal ideals of polynomial rings
(\cref{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}), the height
lower bound at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras
(\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}), the localised
dimension lower bound
(\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}), and the
cotangent upper bound
(\cref{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}) --- which needs no
regular-sequence witness. The Matsumura regular-sequence lemma itself
survives as \cref{lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent}.
The general (non-closed) point case does not require the
localisation theorem for regular local rings (Stacks \texttt{00OF}) at all:
regularity at an arbitrary prime is obtained directly, Serre-free, by the
trdeg--height route of \cref{subsec:smooth_prime_regularity}
(\cref{thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime}).

\subsection{Stacks 00OE closed-point substrate: the Krull-height route}
\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}

At a closed point the localised Krull-dimension formula
(\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}, Stacks 00OE) is established by the

(\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}), the localised dimension

\cref{lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent} supplies the
regular-sequence input when a cotangent basis is available. Regularity at an
arbitrary (not necessarily closed) prime is obtained directly by the
transcendence-degree/height route of \cref{subsec:smooth_prime_regularity}
(\cref{thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime}), independently of the localisation
theorem for regular local rings (Stacks \texttt{00OF}).

data.content:
1640	
1641	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1642	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1643	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1644	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or of *pure codimension 1*,
1645	encoded as: every point of `Z(f)` lies in the closure of a codim-1 point
1646	*of `Z(f)` itself*. (The `z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f` conjunct is essential:
1647	without it the disjunct is nearly vacuous — on a variety every non-generic
1648	point specialises from *some* codim-1 point — and, in particular, too weak
1649	to combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 to force `Z(f) = ∅`
1650	in Theorem 3.2. Statement strengthened in run-0006 T6, session 0015.)
1651	
1652	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1653	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1654	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1655	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1656	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1657	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1658	
1659	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1660	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1661	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1662	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1663	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1664	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1665	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1666	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1667	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1668	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1669	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1670	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1671	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
1672	    (_hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
1673	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1674	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1675	        ∃ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1676	          Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1677	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1678	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1679	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1680	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1681	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1682	  --
1683	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1684	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1685	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1686	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1687	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1688	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1689	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1690	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1691	  --   the domain of definition.
1692	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1693	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1694	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1695	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1696	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1697	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1698	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1699	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1700	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1701	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1702	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1703	  --   not packaged.
1704	  --

1707	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne
1708	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form).

input.content:
# Next-session recommendation (Albanese / T6)

**Target: close Milne 3.3 — the single remaining leaf of the Thm 3.2 extension chain.**
`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` at `CodimOneExtension.lean:1661` (`sorry` at :1709). Once proved, `av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` → `extend_to_av` become sorry-free automatically.

- The in-file docstring (lines 1652–1708) already gives Milne's 4-substep plan. Follow it; do NOT re-derive.
- Two substantive Mathlib gaps to build (both project-side, no Mathlib PR needed):
  - **(a)** the function-field-pullback bridge for `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at :744 — solve once, reuse).
  - **(b)** codim-1 pole-divisor ∩ diagonal is pure codim-1 (Hartshorne AG 9.2 scheme-level; Mathlib has Krull HPP but not this packaging).
- Difference map `Φ:(x,y)↦f(x)·f(y)⁻¹` is built from `GrpObj`/`MonObj.mul` + the project's right-monoidal structure (substep 1); substeps 2–3 are group-law-continuity + openness of the domain.

**Pitfalls / do-nots:**
- **Do NOT weaken the statement.** The `z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f` conjunct is load-bearing — without it the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force `Z(f)=∅` in Thm 3.2. It was deliberately strengthened in session 0015.
- **Do NOT drop the over-k̄ hypothesis** `_hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap`; Milne's maps are k̄-maps and the valuative base-compatibility needs it (see memory [[I-0065]]).
- **Do NOT reintroduce** any `extend_of_codimOneFree`-style "codim-1-free ⇒ extends to complete target" lemma — it is FALSE (ℙ²⇢ℙ¹ counterexample); a Weil-style codim-≥2 extension holds only for group-variety targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is **not** needed anywhere in this chain — it only ever served the removed false statement. Don't re-add the dependency.

**Verified baseline (this reconcile):** Albanese `lake build` green (8582 jobs); `CodimOneExtension`/`Thm32` new lemmas + `SmoothPrimeRegularity` capstone axiom-clean (`propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`); blueprint 0 dangling, 289 proved. Only `sorry` in the T6 files is Milne 3.3.

**Concurrency note:** other runs (T2/T8) build the AJC/Cech libraries concurrently — Albanese is a separate project dir and safe to build in isolation, but don't launch AJC builds from here.
